Politics.headline: Third 'No Kings' protest draws millions from across US to push back on Trump administration
Politics.detail: Millions of people participated in the third 'No Kings' protest across the United States to demonstrate against President Trump and his administration. The protests occurred in cities around the world.

Politics.headline: Keir Starmer says UK will 'have to act' to curb addictive features of social media
Politics.detail: UK's Keir Starmer stated that the UK will need to take action to address the addictive aspects of social media platforms. This comes amid growing concerns about the impact of social media on mental health and society.

Politics.headline: Nepal's ex-PM Oli detained over alleged role in deadly protest crackdown
Politics.detail: Nepal's former Prime Minister Oli has been detained due to his alleged involvement in a deadly crackdown on protests. The details surrounding the charges are still emerging.

Politics.headline: House Republicans shoot down bill that would pay federal airport workers
Politics.detail: House Republicans rejected a bill aimed at paying federal airport workers. A White House memo indicated that Trump instructed DHS to pay federal airport workers as long lines snarl travel.

Politics.headline: FBI director Kash Patel's emails, photos hacked by Iran-linked group
Politics.detail: The personal email account of FBI Director Kash Patel was reportedly hacked by a group with ties to Iran. The group, Handala Hack Team, claimed responsibility and said they accessed Patel's personal email.

Finance & Economy.headline: Fed Chair Jerome Powell Just Sent a Signal That Could Shake the Stock Market
Finance & Economy.detail: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated the central bank is concerned about a potential spike in inflation and may raise interest rates to combat it, which could negatively impact the stock market by making debt more expensive and reducing investor aggressiveness.

Finance & Economy.headline: Finance minister hints at expanding vehicle rotation system to private sector if oil prices further rise
Finance & Economy.detail: South Korea's Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol suggested that the government might extend a mandatory five-day vehicle rotation system to the private sector if global oil prices surge to $120-130 per barrel due to the escalating conflict in the Middle East. This measure aims to conserve energy amidst rising tensions.

Finance & Economy.headline: War in Iran erodes the chancellor's headroom and exposes the UK's fragility
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing war in Iran is impacting the UK economy, reducing the chancellor's financial flexibility. Rising energy costs and potential medicine shortages are key concerns.

Finance & Economy.headline: Economic reform tops agenda as new government sets ambitious growth targets
Finance & Economy.detail: Nepal's new government, led by Prime Minister Balendra Shah and Finance Minister Swarnim Wagle, is prioritizing economic reform with goals of 7% annual growth, raising per capita income above $3,000, and expanding the economy to Rs 10 trillion. The government plans to regulate non-financial sectors, strengthen oversight of financial institutions, and restructure regulatory bodies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial markets fall to new 2026 low as oil rises again
Finance & Economy.detail: Financial markets are declining to new lows in 2026 as oil prices surge, with consumer sentiment dropping to its lowest level since December due to investor, business, and consumer anxieties surrounding the Iran conflict. Rising gas prices are contributing to the declining confidence in the economy.

Technology.headline: Malware Is Sleeping on the Blockchain, and It's Already Infected Dozens of Global Targets
Technology.detail: Experts warn the campaign could outpace past global cyberattacks, with hundreds of thousands of credentials already compromised, as malware finds a new hiding place on the blockchain.

Technology.headline: SpaceX Might Acquire More Spectrum for Starlink Mobile Via FCC Auction
Technology.detail: Despite a nearly $20 billion deal to purchase spectrum from EchoStar last year, SpaceX's name has been spotted on an application document for an upcoming FCC AWS-3 auction, signaling further expansion of Starlink Mobile.

Technology.headline: Sakura Internet formally selected as government cloud provider
Technology.detail: Japan's Digital Agency has formally selected Sakura Internet and four major U.S. companies as service providers for the "government cloud" common information system infrastructure for central and local governments for fiscal 2026, making Sakura Internet the first domestic service provider for the government cloud.

Technology.headline: United's New Upgrades Aim to Keep You Online and Fully Charged at 35,000 Feet
Technology.detail: United Airlines is expanding Starlink access, adding OLED 4K screens, and including new power ports to improve the flying experience and keep passengers connected and charged.

Technology.headline: The "Artificial Intelligence + Industry" Forum 2026: AI Facilitates Industrial Transformation
Technology.detail: The forum focused on the large-scale commercial application of AI, fostering new business models and formats native to AI, deepening the development and utilization of data resources, and improving AI governance. Experts at the forum shared insights on how AI is empowering the automated system of manufacturing, heralding the advent of "super-automated factories".

Science.headline: This Quantum Computing Breakthrough May Not Be What It Seemed
Science.detail: A team of physicists set out to test some of the most exciting claims in quantum computing and found a very different story, revealing that signals once hailed as major advances didn't hold up in replication studies.

Science.headline: Scientists Discover Ocean 'Missing' Plastic Has Broken Down Into Nanoplastics
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that the ocean's “missing” plastic hasn't vanished but has broken down into trillions of invisible nanoplastics now spread through water, air, and living organisms.

Science.headline: Stroke Triggers a Hidden Brain Change That Looks Like Rejuvenation
Science.detail: After a stroke, the brain may do something surprisingly hopeful, researchers analyzing brain scans from over 500 stroke survivors found that while the damaged side of the brain appears to age faster, the brain can “refresh” parts of itself.

Science.headline: World's Smallest QR Code, Smaller Than Bacteria, Could Store Data for Centuries
Science.detail: Scientists have created a microscopic QR code so tiny it can only be seen with an electron microscope—smaller than most bacteria and now officially a world record.

Science.headline: This New Therapy Turns Off Pain Without Opioids or Addiction
Science.detail: A new therapy has been developed that turns off pain without the use of opioids or causing addiction.

Health.headline: What teens eat could be affecting their mental health more than we thought
Health.detail: A review of studies found that healthier diets are often linked to fewer depressive symptoms, while poor eating habits may go hand in hand with greater psychological distress, suggesting that overall eating habits could play a meaningful role during this critical stage of brain development.

Health.headline: Ohio, Michigan strive to overcome shortage of mental health care workers
Health.detail: Ohio and Michigan are facing a shortage of mental health care workers, leading to patients seeking out-of-network care or going without care. Worker burnout during the pandemic has depleted the workforce, even as rates of depression and anxiety spike.

Health.headline: Minister Butler, Adelaide - 29 March 2026
Health.detail: The Australian government is adding the immunotherapy drug Keytruda to the Pharmaceutical Benefits Scheme (PBS) for locally advanced cervical cancer, head and neck cancer, and kidney cancers to improve patient outcomes. According to Dr Sukumaran, for all three cancers, this is a major advancement that has improved outcomes.
Health.source: Australian Government Department of Health and Aged Care

Health.headline: Mental health issues plague new moms in the military. The Pentagon is supposed to help
Health.detail: Delays in implementing the MOMS Act, intended to address perinatal mental health conditions in the military, may reflect the military's lack of interest in women's and family issues. Representative Chrissy Houlahan stated that the Defense Health Agency is coordinating on a report to Congress detailing activities to address perinatal mental health conditions.

Health.headline: Carolyn Hax: Stepdad beat cancer, and he's now bringing family to heel
Health.detail: A letter writer is worried about their mother who “completely defers” to her husband, who has clamped down on everyone since his cancer treatment. The writer is dreading visiting their mom because of the stepfather's behavior, and Carolyn Hax advises that the mother needs help from her adult children.

Sports.headline: Aryna Sabalenka beats Coco Gauff in the Miami Open final to complete 'Sunshine Double'
Sports.detail: Aryna Sabalenka defeated Coco Gauff in the Miami Open final, achieving the 'Sunshine Double'.

Sports.headline: Flames ignite their offense, burn Canucks 7-3
Sports.detail: The Calgary Flames' offense exploded in a 7-3 victory over the Vancouver Canucks.

Sports.headline: Quin Snyder earns 500th career win as Hawks notch their 15th victory in 17 games
Sports.detail: Quin Snyder achieved his 500th career win as the Atlanta Hawks secured their 15th victory in the last 17 games.

Sports.headline: 2026 UFL Week 1 Results: Stallions, Renegades, Battlehawks Win Big
Sports.detail: The Stallions, Renegades, and Battlehawks all recorded significant victories in Week 1 of the 2026 UFL season.

Sports.headline: Guardians' Chase DeLauter hits 4th home run in 3rd career game, Guardians beat Mariners 6-5
Sports.detail: Chase DeLauter continued his hot start to his career by hitting his 4th home run in his 3rd game as the Guardians beat the Mariners 6-5.

Entertainment.headline: Tiger Woods' Mugshot 2026: See the Photo of the Golfer After His DUI Arrest
Entertainment.detail: Tiger Woods' mugshot was released to the public shortly after he was arrested and charged with suspicion of DUI.

Entertainment.headline: What Does the 'No Kings' Protest Mean? Learn About it Here
Entertainment.detail: Across the United States, the viral 'No Kings' protest took place on March 28, 2026, and this article explains why it matters and provides information about the next nationwide protest.

Entertainment.headline: 'Love Story' Season 2: Everything We Know So Far About the Cast, Story & More
Entertainment.detail: Following its first season about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, here's what to know about a potential 'Love Story' season 2, from the cast to the story and release updates.

Entertainment.headline: Stars Who Survived Car Crashes: Tiger Woods, Scott Disick & More Celebrity Car Accidents
Entertainment.detail: This article looks at celebrities who have had luck on their side after surviving some nasty car accidents in the past.

Entertainment.headline: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 11 [LIVE]: Ranveer Singh film crosses Rs 820 crore net
Entertainment.detail: The Ranveer Singh film 'Dhurandhar 2' has crossed Rs 820 crore net at the box office.

Crime & Justice.headline: French police thwart suspected bombing outside Bank of America in Paris
Crime & Justice.detail: French police prevented a potential bomb attack near a Bank of America building in Paris, detaining one suspect while another fled; the national anti-terrorism prosecutor's office has launched a terrorism-related investigation.

Crime & Justice.headline: Editorial: End of mandatory life sentence for felony murder restores real justice in Pennsylvania
Crime & Justice.detail: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court ruled to end the mandatory sentence of life without parole for felony murder, allowing courts to consider the circumstances of the crime when applying a sentence. This decision removes Pennsylvania from the list of states that unfairly impose this mandatory sentence.

Crime & Justice.headline: Meet The Drone Pilot Who Flooded Ohio Prisons With Drugs
Crime & Justice.detail: Cory Sutphin, an Ohio drone pilot, operated a drug delivery service, like DoorDash, inside Ohio prisons before getting caught. He taught himself how to fly drones and made around $100,000 in just seven months smuggling drugs, cell phones, and other contraband.

Crime & Justice.headline: Criminal Justice Reform Still Alive, Despite Federal Setbacks
Crime & Justice.detail: Criminal justice reform in the United States is becoming more decentralized, with many states continuing to pass reform laws even with federal setbacks. In 2025, 35 states passed at least one reform law, and the number of reform laws enacted grew from 96 in 2024 to 115 in 2025.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ohio Prisons Overrun With Drugs
Crime & Justice.detail: Ohio prisons are facing a significant drug problem, with synthetic drugs like K2 being smuggled in by staff, visitors, and drones.  Despite efforts to increase security, the smuggling continues, leading to overdoses and other health issues among incarcerated individuals.

Education.headline: Krishna Gupta Tops Gopalganj In Bihar Class 10 Exam With 96 per cent
Education.detail: Krishna Gupta, son of a security guard, has topped the Bihar Class 10 exam in Gopalganj with 96%. The Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) announced the results for Class 10 on March 29, 2026.

Education.headline: Tyre Repair Shop Owner's Daughter Sabreen Parveen Tops With 98.4%
Education.detail: Sabreen Parveen, daughter of a tyre repair shop owner, has topped the Bihar BSEB Class 10 exam with 98.4%. The results were declared on March 29, 2026.

Education.headline: Bihar BSEB Class 10 Result 2026: Pushpanjali Kumari Tops With 98.4%, Aspires To Become Scientist
Education.detail: Pushpanjali Kumari has topped the Bihar BSEB Class 10 Result 2026 with 98.4% and aspires to become a scientist. The results were announced on March 29, 2026.

Education.headline: Bihar 10th Board Results 2026: Aurangabad's Shiv Kumar Secures 10th Rank
Education.detail: Shiv Kumar from Aurangabad secured the 10th rank in the Bihar 10th Board Results 2026. The results were declared on March 29, 2026.

Education.headline: UGC Flags Rajasthan-Based Institute As Fake University, Full List Here
Education.detail: The University Grants Commission (UGC) has identified and flagged an institute in Rajasthan as a fake university. The announcement was made on March 29, 2026.

Lifestyle.headline: Long lines, martinis and memories as LA says adieu to cherished restaurant Taix
Lifestyle.detail: After serving the community for decades, the Los Angeles restaurant Taix has closed its doors, leaving behind a legacy of memories and good food.

Lifestyle.headline: 'I thought, what the hell have I done?': the people who moved abroad for love – and regretted it
Lifestyle.detail: This article shares personal stories of individuals who relocated to foreign countries for love, only to face unexpected challenges and regret their decision, highlighting the complexities of making such a significant life change for a relationship.

Lifestyle.headline: New life for old shoes
Lifestyle.detail: A French nonprofit called SneakCoeurZ is working to reduce fashion waste by collecting, sorting, and either reselling or redistributing used sneakers. The organization aims to industrialize the collection and reuse of sneakers and collected 30,000 pairs of used sneakers last year and resold 2,000 pairs.

Lifestyle.headline: Pic story: former serf's new life in China's Xizang
Lifestyle.detail: This article likely showcases the improved living conditions and lifestyle of a person in China's Xizang region who was once a serf. The story probably focuses on the positive changes and developments in their life.

Lifestyle.headline: Dubai Airports Update March 29, 2026: Air India Group to run 30 flights; IndiGo says “we stand with you to ensure your travel plans remain as smooth as possible”
Lifestyle.detail: Despite regional tensions and conflict in the Gulf, Air India and Air India Express will operate 30 flights to and from West Asia, and IndiGo is working to ensure travel plans remain smooth. Passengers are advised to confirm flights, stay connected with airlines, have travel documents ready, and allow extra time for security checks.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Shadow of crisis
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The IMF appears to acknowledge that exogenous shocks of this magnitude require calibrated flexibility rather than rigid adherence to doctrine.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Narrowing space
Opinion/Editorial.detail: THE HRCP report Regulation or Restriction? is a telling account of the constraints faced by NGOs in Punjab.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Hotter urban future
Opinion/Editorial.detail: PAKISTAN is headed for a dangerous future, one that is not only characterised by rapid urbanisation but also rising...

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: To understand homelessness, listen to homeless people. Here's what I learned
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial opinion and analysis from the L.A. Times Editorial Board regarding understanding homelessness by listening to those affected.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on falling donations to charity: rising living costs are part of the problem, but not all of it
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial from The Guardian discussing the factors behind declining charitable donations, including the impact of increasing living expenses.

World.headline: More than 3,500 U.S. troops arrive in Middle East as Iran war intensifies
World.detail: U.S. Central Command reported that over 11,000 targets have been struck since Operation Epic Fury began on Feb. 28, as the war with Iran escalates.

World.headline: Americans struggle as Iran war puts strain on everyday costs: 'I'm worried we won't make it through'
World.detail: The ongoing war in Iran is causing financial strain for many Americans, impacting their daily lives and raising concerns about their ability to cope.

World.headline: Pakistan hosts four-nation bid to encourage US, Iran towards diplomacy
World.detail: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Turkiye, Egypt and Saudi Arabia are holding talks in Islamabad to encourage the US and Iran towards a diplomatic resolution to the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Yemen's Houthis enter Iran war as bloodshed mounts daily across region
World.detail: Houthi forces in Yemen have joined the conflict, launching missile attacks on Israeli military sites, further escalating the already volatile situation in the region.

World.headline: Nestlé says 413,793 KitKat candy bars stolen en route from Italy to Poland
World.detail: A large quantity of KitKat candy bars was stolen while being transported from Italy to Poland, according to Nestlé.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF Mashonaland West leadership psyches supporters ahead of CAB3 public hearings this week.
Zimbabwe.detail: Zanu PF Mashonaland West is preparing its supporters for the upcoming public hearings on the controversial Constitution Amendment Bill (CAB3). The party aims to rally support for the bill, which proposes extending presidential and parliamentary terms.

Zimbabwe.headline: ZimParks issues trip advisory on Mana Pools as rains damage bridges, roads.
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe Parks and Wildlife Management Authority (ZimParks) has issued a travel advisory for Mana Pools National Park due to damage to bridges and roads caused by recent heavy rains.

Zimbabwe.headline: Member of Parliament in dock for diverting CDF money to companies linked to himself.
Zimbabwe.detail: A Member of Parliament has been arrested and is facing charges for allegedly diverting Constituency Development Fund (CDF) money to companies in which he has a vested interest.

Zimbabwe.headline: Warriors crush Botswana in Four Nations tournament opener.
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe's national football team, the Warriors, secured a victory against Botswana in the opening match of the Four Nations tournament.

Zimbabwe.headline: Law Society of Zimbabwe unveils new identity.
Zimbabwe.detail: The Law Society of Zimbabwe (LSZ) has launched a rebranding exercise, which signals a shift in its operations and public image.
